facts.excerpt: MiniMax spent about 142 million US dollars (about 1 billion yuan) on computing - service expenses related to training in the first three quarters of this year.
facts.notes: Rented compute (opex, not owned-facility capex). Figure is training-related computing-service spend for first three quarters of 2025, from MiniMax IPO prospectus as reported by 36Kr. IPO prospectus separately reports Alibaba Cloud spend of ~$40k (2022), $3.1M (2023), $10M (2024), $58.4M (9M2025) and future purchase caps $115M/$125M/$135M for 2026/2027/2028 - but I could not fetch a page carrying those verbatim (itiger.com 403), so those specific figures are search-summary only and unverified. date_published approximate. Translation/secondary-source uncertainty.

verification.verifier_note: The $142M number and period ARE on the page: 'MiniMax spent about 142 million US dollars (about 1 billion yuan) on computing-service expenses related to training in the first three quarters of this year' (Q1-Q3 2025, i.e. through 2025-09-30). BUT the page attributes this to general 'computing-servic

facts.value: Light-asset strategy: MiniMax does not own training clusters and has no meaningful local compute; rents from a diverse set of cloud suppliers, including Chinese firms and Singapore-based providers, beyond Alibaba Cloud.
facts.excerpt: MiniMax "does not have its own training clusters and has no meaningful local compute"; the prospectus lists diverse cloud suppliers, some Chinese and some Singapore-based.
facts.notes: Source domain chinatalk.media is NOT on the preferred list (Tier-B analysis of the HK IPO prospectus) - flag for review. Reinforces that MiniMax owns no datacenters/GPU clusters. Suppliers are anonymized A-K in the prospectus; only Alibaba Cloud is publicly identified. Article publish date approximate (early Jan 2026, around the Jan 9 2026 HK listing) - date uncertain. Secondary-source / English-summary-of-Chinese-filing uncertainty.

verification.verifier_note: from gap-review pass; pending blind verification
